title: [InstCombine] Try to fold trunc(shuffle(zext)) to just a shuffle
summary: The developers generalized a pattern for evaluating sign bits through shuffles by extending `EvaluateInDifferentType` and `canEvaluateTruncated` to handle `ShuffleVectorInst`. The original test case involved repeated shuffles and ORs to compute a sign mask, which was then used to select between bitcasted vectors. The key insight was that sign-bit evaluation (via `sext` to a wider type) can be pushed through shuffles without altering the shuffle mask, since shuffles only rearrange elements. By adding support for `ShuffleVector` in both functions, the transformation now works for any shuffle that feeds into a sign-extended condition, including multi-input shuffles. This allows the optimization to recognize and simplify more complex sign-bit propagation patterns, reducing redundant operations. Developers can apply this approach by ensuring that type-agnostic operations (like shuffles) are recursively supported in evaluation helpers, enabling broader pattern matching across composite expressions.
